alignments.candidate_evidence: Eight years at Google scaling global commercial operations and managing transformation programs with cross-functional ownership
alignments.job_requirement: Global cross-functional partnership and stakeholder management across governments and regulators

alignments.candidate_evidence: Product Operations and Strategy Operations background with transformation program leadership, not government affairs, public policy, regulatory, or advocacy experience
alignments.job_requirement: Voice representation on industry-shaping issues and policy landscape

gaps.gap: No government affairs, public policy, regulatory affairs, or legislative advocacy background; candidate is ops/strategy from technology product side
gaps.mitigation: Government affairs is a specialized function requiring domain expertise in policy frameworks, regulatory strategy, and advocacy—areas not present in candidate's operations-focused background

gaps.gap: Head of title and regional P&L ownership scope exceed L4–L5 target; established company tier-2 headship roles are over-leveled
gaps.mitigation: Candidate's scope ceiling is Manager to Senior Manager / Lead level; this is a country/region leadership role

gaps.gap: No creatives/music industry, media policy, competition law, online safety, or AI regulation domain knowledge
gaps.mitigation: Role requires deep familiarity with Spotify's creator ecosystem, music licensing, and regulatory landscape—candidate's expertise is in device/services operations

gaps.gap: No demonstrated external affairs, lobbyying, or regulatory liaison experience
gaps.mitigation: External stakeholder management differs materially from internal cross-functional operations and program leadership

output.summary: Candidate is a Strategy and Operations leader with strong global commercial-operations and transformation-program experience at Google, but this role is a Government Affairs regional headship—a specialized policy and advocacy function completely outside the candidate's domain. Title scope (Head of) and company tier exceed the L4–L5 ceiling, and there is no government affairs, regulatory, public policy, or music-industry background. This is a domain mismatch and over-leveled role; skip.
